title: Smoky, Crispy British Chicken Hash
description: A delightful one-pot British dish featuring smoky chicken, crispy potatoes, and caramelized onions, all made with buttermilk for tender and moist results.

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally, about 15 minutes.
Add the diced potatoes to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Pour in the buttermilk and add the smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until the chicken is cooked through and the potatoes are tender, about 10-12 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Use a spatula to break up the chicken into small pieces and stir to combine with the potatoes and onions.
Increase the heat to high and cook until the bottom of the hash is crispy and golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Use a spatula to transfer the hash to a serving platter and serve hot.
Garnish with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
